priority.title: SEC filing: 8-K — Fermi Inc. (CURRENT REPORT)
priority.formExplanation: A current report — the form companies use to disclose material events between quarters (deals, leadership changes, results, financings).
priority.summary: Fermi Inc. has appointed four new officers, including George Wentz as General Counsel, Anna Bofa as Chief Commercial Officer, Jacobo Ortiz as Chief Operating Officer, and Rob Masson as Chief Financial Officer, with their appointments taking effect on July 22, 2026. The new officers will receive annualized base salaries ranging from $500,000 to $650,000, as well as potential bonuses and severance packages, and will participate in the company's 2025 Long-Term Incentive Plan with awards having an aggregate grant date value of up to $3 million. The appointments and associated compensation packages were approved by the company's Board of Directors.

priority.title: SEC filing: 8-K/A — Fermi Inc. (AMENDMENT NO. 1 TO FORM 8-K)
priority.formExplanation: A current report — the form companies use to disclose material events between quarters (deals, leadership changes, results, financings).
priority.summary: Fermi Inc. discloses the immediate resignation of director Miles Everson, effective July 10, 2026, and also includes a response letter from Everson regarding the company's previous 8-K filing. The response letter is attached as an exhibit to this amended filing. This amendment provides additional context to Everson's resignation and the company's subsequent reporting.

priority.title: SEC filing: SCHEDULE 13G — Fermi Inc.
priority.formExplanation: An SEC filing — primary-source disclosure straight from EDGAR.
priority.summary: An SEC filing — primary-source disclosure straight from EDGAR.

priority.title: SEC filing: 8-K — Fermi Inc. (CURRENT REPORT)
priority.formExplanation: A current report — the form companies use to disclose material events between quarters (deals, leadership changes, results, financings).
priority.summary: Fermi Inc. has issued $431.25 million in aggregate principal amount of 5.00% convertible senior notes due 2031, with total net proceeds of approximately $416.81 million, which will be used for general corporate purposes. The notes are convertible into cash, shares of common stock, or a combination thereof, at the company's election, with an initial conversion price of approximately $9.52 per share. The company also entered into capped call transactions to reduce potential dilution to its common stock upon conversion of the notes, using approximately $34.5 million of the net proceeds to pay for these transactions.

priority.title: SEC filing: 8-K — Fermi Inc. (CURRENT REPORT)
priority.formExplanation: A current report — the form companies use to disclose material events between quarters (deals, leadership changes, results, financings).
priority.summary: Fermi Inc. discloses the immediate resignation of director Miles Everson, effective July 10, 2026, due to a disagreement over access to company books and records and the delegation of financing transaction oversight to the Finance Committee. Everson's resignation was submitted in a letter, which is attached to the filing as an exhibit, and he did not serve on any committee of the Board at the time of his resignation. The company believes Everson was granted full access to its books and records, except for certain litigation-related materials, and had previously approved the establishment of the Finance Committee.

priority.title: SEC filing: 8-K — Fermi Inc. (CURRENT REPORT)
priority.formExplanation: A current report — the form companies use to disclose material events between quarters (deals, leadership changes, results, financings).
priority.summary: Fermi Inc. commenced an offering for the sale of $350 million aggregate principal amount of convertible senior notes due 2031 to qualified institutional buyers, with the option to purchase an additional $52.5 million. The company intends to use the net proceeds to pay for capped call transactions and for general corporate purposes. Fermi Inc. also disclosed that it has deferred its election to be taxed as a real estate investment trust, and now expects to make this election for its taxable year ending December 31, 2026.

priority.title: SEC filing: DFAN14A — Fermi Inc. (DEFINITIVE ADDITIONAL MATERIALS)
priority.formExplanation: Proxy-fight material from a non-management participant — solicitation filings made outside the company's own proxy (in a control contest, each side's letters and presentations land here).
priority.summary: Toby R. Neugebauer, Vicksburg Investments Management LLC, and other parties have withdrawn their solicitation to call a special meeting of Fermi Inc. shareholders due to an untenable timeline caused by a judge's recusal in pending litigation. The special meeting was intended to address entrenching actions taken by the company's directors, but the parties will not act on any agent designations received from shareholders unless the solicitation is resumed. The withdrawal comes after the parties had filed a definitive proxy statement and accompanying agent designation card with the Securities and Exchange Commission.

priority.title: SEC filing: DFAN14A — Fermi Inc. (DEFINITIVE ADDITIONAL MATERIALS)
priority.formExplanation: Proxy-fight material from a non-management participant — solicitation filings made outside the company's own proxy (in a control contest, each side's letters and presentations land here).
priority.summary: Toby R. Neugebauer, co-founder and largest shareholder of Fermi Inc., has suspended his current proxy campaign to call a special meeting of shareholders due to a judge's last-minute recusal, which has disrupted the timeline for the strategic process. Neugebauer's campaign had earned the backing of over 70% of votes cast and two leading proxy advisory firms, and he remains confident in Fermi's ability to secure a tenant group and achieve long-term success. Neugebauer will continue to push for good governance through the judicial process and monitor the board's execution on Project Matador to ensure maximum value for stakeholders.

priority.title: SEC filing: DFAN14A — Fermi Inc. (DEFINITIVE ADDITIONAL MATERIALS)
priority.formExplanation: Proxy-fight material from a non-management participant — solicitation filings made outside the company's own proxy (in a control contest, each side's letters and presentations land here).
priority.summary: Toby R. Neugebauer, co-founder of Fermi America, delivered an investor presentation on June 30, 2026, expressing concerns about the company's contracts, including the EPC contract and gas contract, which he believes could be either assets or liabilities. Neugebauer emphasized the importance of having the right team to steward these contracts and ensure the company's success, and he advocated for a dual-track process and evidence-based decision-making in evaluating potential tenants. Neugebauer also stated that he and his team are not trying to come back to the company, but rather want to ensure that the board prioritizes the interests of shareholders.

priority.title: SEC filing: DFAN14A — Fermi Inc. (DEFINITIVE ADDITIONAL MATERIALS)

priority.summary: Toby R. Neugebauer and other participants, including Vicksburg Investments Management LLC and Melissa A. Neugebauer 2020 Trust, have filed a definitive proxy statement with the SEC in connection with soliciting agent designations for a special meeting of Fermi Inc. shareholders. The filing discloses that Mr. Neugebauer beneficially owns 146,516,035 shares of Fermi Inc.'s common stock, while the other participants do not beneficially own any shares. The participants are soliciting support for calling a special meeting of shareholders to be held as promptly as practicable.

Neugebauer and affiliated entities have filed a definitive proxy statement with the SEC in connection with the solicitation of agent designations for calling a special meeting of Fermi Inc. shareholders. Neugebauer beneficially owns 146,516,035 shares of Fermi's common stock, and is advising all shareholders to read the definitive proxy statement and other proxy materials because they contain important information. priority.title: SEC filing: DFAN14A — Fermi Inc. (DEFINITIVE ADDITIONAL MATERIALS)
priority.formExplanation: Proxy-fight material from a non-management participant — solicitation filings made outside the company's own proxy (in a control contest, each side's letters and presentations land here).
priority.summary: Toby R. Neugebauer, co-founder and largest shareholder of Fermi Inc., has announced that the Texas Business Court granted his motion for expedited discovery, requiring Fermi to provide documentation and testimony regarding its recent actions, including the imposition of a 70% supermajority voting requirement. This development is a significant win for Neugebauer and Fermi's shareholders, who have been seeking greater accountability and transparency from the company's board. Neugebauer will also host a Town Hall webinar with analysts on June 30 to address questions and provide updates on his efforts to restore "good governance" to Fermi.

priority.summary: Toby R. Neugebauer, co-founder and largest individual shareholder of Fermi America, Inc., has proposed a town hall webinar for June 30th where he, Governor Rick Perry, and other parties will answer questions from analysts and journalists about the company's past, present, and future. Neugebauer is seeking to give shareholders a say in the company's direction, particularly regarding Project Matador, a 7,500-acre private power campus in Texas, and whether to pursue a strategic dual-track process to maximize shareholder value. The proposed webinar comes after Governor Perry and the board gave themselves a 70% supermajority vote threshold, making it difficult for shareholders to change course.

priority.title: SEC filing: DEFA14A — Fermi Inc. (SOLICITING MATERIAL PURSUANT TO SECTION 240.14A-12)
priority.formExplanation: Additional definitive proxy material from the company — its side of an ongoing solicitation (responses, letters to shareholders, talking points).
priority.summary: Fermi Inc. discloses that its former CEO Toby Neugebauer is attempting to call a Special Meeting of Shareholders to replace the Board and leadership team, which the company believes would jeopardize shareholder value. The company urges shareholders to sign and return the WHITE Consent Revocation Card to prevent Neugebauer from gaining control, citing his history of false claims and inaccurate statements. Fermi Inc. also highlights its recent commercial momentum and progress under new management, with a 45% increase in stock price since Neugebauer's termination.

priority.title: SEC filing: DFAN14A — Fermi Inc. (DEFINITIVE ADDITIONAL MATERIALS)
priority.formExplanation: Proxy-fight material from a non-management participant — solicitation filings made outside the company's own proxy (in a control contest, each side's letters and presentations land here).
priority.summary: Toby Neugebauer and his affiliated entities, collectively known as the Fermi Founder Parties, are soliciting shareholders to designate agents to call a special meeting of Fermi Inc. shareholders, with the goal of allowing shareholders to hear proposals and decide on the company's future. The Fermi Founder Parties, who collectively own 146,516,035 shares of Fermi Inc. common stock, are urging shareholders to consent to calling the special meeting by voting their GREEN voting instruction forms. Shareholders who own shares through a bank, broker, or other nominee can vote online, while those who hold shares in registered name must sign and return a physical GREEN agent designation card.

related.title: Google Cloud CEO Kurian says customers are spending 50% more as segment blows away expectations

related.title: Nvidia Signs $1.5 Billion Accord With Amkor for Chip Packaging

related.title: Moonshot AI accessed Nvidia's chips despite Chinese export ban, White House official says
related.summary: A White House official said Moonshot AI, which released the powerful Kimi K3 model last week, accessed Nvidia's GB300 chips in Thailand.

related.title: Google Fined €890 Million by EU Over Search, Play Store Breaches

related.title: Reddit stock sinks on report it may not renew Google AI content deal
related.summary: Google's AI summaries have reduced traffic to websites from the search results page and Reddit is reconsidering the benefits of the deal, the Journal said.

related.title: OpenAI cyber models broke out of training environment to hack Hugging Face
related.summary: The incident is unique because it was "driven, end to end, by an autonomous AI agent system," according to Hugging Face.

general.title: Wheat Extends July Rally as War Risks Stoke Fears Over Supply
general.summary: Wheat resumed its rally after a brief bout of profit-taking, as escalating geopolitical tensions and worsening weather in key producing regions continued to stoke supply concerns.

general.title: U.S., other nations back open-source AI with 'strong security' at China summit

general.title: Malaysia Sells First Dollar Bonds in Five Years as Fuel Subsidy Bill Rises
general.summary: Malaysia raised $1.5 billion in its first dollar bond sale in five years, bolstering funding as the Southeast Asian nation grapples with a fuel subsidy bill that’s likely to more than double from an initial goal due to the Iran war.

general.title: India’s SEBI Mulls Letting Portfolio Managers Invest Clients’ Money Overseas
general.summary: SEBI proposes allowing investment in overseas securities and IPO-bound firms, among others.

general.title: Manipal Health Seeks to Raise Up to $960 Million in India IPO

general.title: Asian Allies Push Back Against Trump’s Forced-Labor Tariffs
general.summary: Asian nations have described the Trump administration’s latest tariffs as baseless and unjustified, while stopping short of any retaliatory moves.

general.title: China's largest memory chipmaker sparks fears of a cash drain as it readies for public debut
general.summary: CXMT is stoking fears that its blockbuster stock market debut could pull cash from China's equities.

general.title: China’s Gold Imports Surge After International Prices Slump
general.summary: Chinese gold imports rose to a two-year high in June, underscoring resilient demand in the world’s biggest bullion market after a plunge in international prices.

general.title: Chinese Stock Turnover Shrinks as Sentiment Cools on Tech Sector
general.summary: Chinese daily stock turnover dropped to the lowest level in over three months, as investors shun the nation’s technology sector.

general.title: Japan’s Katayama Says to Take Bold FX Steps Decisively as Needed
general.summary: Japanese authorities will take bold steps to counter moves in the currency market decisively as needed, and a US Treasury report issued overnight underscores a shared view that excessive currency moves are undesirable, Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama said.

general.title: Trump to Take Up Manila’s Concerns With Xi, Philippines Says
general.summary: US President Donald Trump assured Philippine leader Ferdinand Marcos Jr. that he would take up Manila’s concerns when he meets with Chinese President Xi Jinping, the Southeast Asian nation said.

general.title: SK Hynix, Samsung and Kioxia Face Pivotal Earnings Test on AI Swings
general.summary: SK Hynix Inc. and Samsung Electronics Co. are set to test investor appetite for memory chips as South Korea’s stock market — now a bellwether for global AI sentiment — grapples with violent swings driven by leveraged chip bets.

general.title: PHP/USD: Philippine Peso Falls to Fresh Record Low as Oil Advances
general.summary: The Philippine peso fell to a fresh record low as oil prices climbed, boosting pressure on the central bank to intervene as markets eye 62 as the next key level.

general.title: Macquarie’s First Woman CEO Departs With a $339 Million Stake
general.summary: Shemara Wikramanayake is stepping down as head of Macquarie Group Ltd. after leading the Australian financial conglomerate to bumper profits and a record high share price. For that she will be richly rewarded, making her one of the best-paid female bank executives in the world.

general.title: Modi Seeks to Calm Anger in Rare Video as India Protests Widen

general.title: PBOC Adds Most Liquidity to Economy in Five Months to Aid Growth

general.title: Singapore Pushes Back as US Levies Hit on Forced-Labor Claim

general.title: Top Environmental Fund Sees Japan Key to AI Energy Challenge

general.title: S. Korea Moves Up Leveraged ETF Deposit Rule to Tame Volatility
general.summary: South Korea is moving up the date it had planned to lift the minimum cash deposit requirement for investors in leveraged exchange-traded funds to July 31, a measure to curb demand for the products that are blamed for amplifying stock volatility.

general.title: Intel's turnaround under CEO Lip-Bu Tan gains steam with another strong quarter

general.title: Japan’s Inflation Picks Up, Keeping BOJ on Path for Rate Hikes

general.title: UK Consumer Confidence Recovers to Pre-War Levels, GfK Says
general.summary: Britons put the Iran war energy shock behind them this month as the soccer World Cup and Prime Minister Andy Burnham’s promise to help with living costs lifted the national mood, according to a survey.

general.title: Singapore MAS Set to Hold on Mild Inflation, Signal Tightening
general.summary: Singapore’s subdued inflation will likely allow the central bank to maintain its monetary policy settings on Monday, while it assesses the potential impact of a resurgence in the US-Iran conflict.

general.title: IBM's Krishna tries to reassure investors that AI won't disrupt company's software unit
general.summary: IBM's CEO highlighted growing mainframe hardware capacity, and said software should "catch back up" within a year.

general.title: Robotics Startup Genesis in Talks to Raise About $500 Million
general.summary: AI robotics startup Genesis AI is in talks with investors to raise around $500 million in a new funding round, according to people familiar with the efforts.

general.title: Intel's stock jumps as chipmaker rides AI boom to fastest revenue growth in almost 15 years
general.summary: Intel rallied after the company reported blowout earnings and guidance.

general.title: Historic Yen Slide Poses New Risk to Japan Stocks’ Bull Run
general.summary: The yen’s relentless slide is raising concern that it risks eventually curbing the foreign inflows that have helped propel Japanese equities to record highs.

general.title: Stocks Fall as Tech Selloff Extends, Oil Near $100: Markets Wrap
general.summary: Stocks and bonds fell as doubts over returns from billions of dollars spent on artificial intelligence coincided with a surge in oil prices that rekindled inflation concerns.

general.title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 24
general.summary: Brent crude traded near $100 a barrel after Houthi attacks on tankers in the Red Sea opened a new front in the Middle East conflict, while President Donald Trump threatened to extend US strikes on Iran.

general.title: Oracle signs 10-year software contract with Pentagon worth up to $7 billion
general.summary: Oracle will supply on-premises software to the U.S. Department of Defense.

general.title: SK Hynix Billionaire to Get Long-Awaited Divorce Case Ruling
general.summary: SK Hynix Inc. billionaire Chey Tae-won, whose fortune has been supercharged by the AI-boom, is set to receive a fresh court ruling in his long-running divorce battle with his former wife, bringing the acrimonious dispute closer to an end.

general.title: Tech Bonds Hit by Selloff as AI Debt Fears Race Through Markets
general.summary: The bonds of some of the biggest US tech companies slid Thursday amid renewed worries about the scale of the debt-fueled artificial-intelligence boom and escalating conflict in the Middle East.

general.title: Cerebras stock gains on AMD partnership
general.summary: Cerebras and AMD agreed to work together, pairing their technology for AI systems.

general.title: US Treasury Calls Out China for Lack of Transparency on Yuan
general.summary: China stands out among US trading partners for its “relative lack of transparency” around currency management, the Treasury Department said in a semiannual foreign-exchange report released Thursday.

general.title: Tesla, Alphabet lose hundreds of billions in value in post-earnings stock plunge
general.summary: Both Tesla and Alphabet signaled higher spending as they invest in artificial intelligence.

general.title: American Airlines stock tumbles 8% as fuel spike further postpones turnaround
general.summary: American Airlines further cut its 2026 earnings outlook, citing higher fuel costs.

general.title: SAP Cloud Revenue Beats Estimates as Legacy Support Nears Cutoff
general.summary: SAP SE’s cloud revenue grew at a faster pace than analysts expected as customers move away from on-premises licenses ahead of the company’s deadline to halt support for legacy software.

general.title: Intel’s AI-Fueled Forecast Shows Comeback Is Gaining Steam
general.summary: Intel Corp. delivered a revenue forecast that shattered Wall Street estimates as booming data center spending fuels a long-awaited turnaround.

general.title: Alphabet’s Anthropic Stake Jumps to Around $124 Billion
general.summary: The value of Alphabet Inc.’s stake in the artificial intelligence startup Anthropic PBC has soared to around $124 billion, making it one of the most lucrative bets in the company’s history.

general.title: Trump Expands AI Data Center Pledge in Bid to Ease Power Costs
general.summary: President Donald Trump on Thursday welcomed fresh commitments from power utilities and data center developers to put technology companies on the hook to pay for the electricity they need to drive energy-hungry artificial-intelligence systems.

general.title: How Houthis’ Red Sea Attacks Worsen Oil Shock
general.summary: One thing that’s helped limit the Iran war’s disruption to global oil supply is Saudi Arabia’s ability to use an alternative export route to the Strait of Hormuz: the Red Sea.

general.title: A Group of Industry Powerbrokers Are Unveiling a New Definition of “Podcast”
general.summary: After months of secret meetings, a group of industry heavyweights are releasing a white paper codifying new podcasting frameworks

general.title: US to Produce Ukraine’s Magura Sea Drones for the First Time
general.summary: A US manufacturer is set to produce Ukraine-designed, battle-tested boat drones for the first time, as the White House bids to bolster America’s military production and expand its unmanned arsenal.

general.title: We're raising our Honeywell Technologies price target, reflecting post-spinoff optimism
general.summary: Honeywell Technologies bucked a broader market selloff after the industrial automation company delivered a strong quarter and raised guidance.

general.title: JPMorgan report finds dramatic jump in AI-themed ETFs — despite rough quarter
general.summary: Wall Street is banking heavily on exchange-traded funds that give investors artificial intelligence exposure, according to JPMorgan Asset Management.
